name: institutional_and_social_trust
description: Measures the degree to which individuals trust formal institutions (police) and informal social actors (neighbors, strangers), capturing generalized interpersonal and institutional confidence that underpins civic cooperation.

name: national_identity_and_pride
description: Captures the emotional and symbolic attachment respondents feel toward Mexico as a nation, including pride in being Mexican, primary emotions about the country, and the importance of preserving cultural traditions of origin.

name: subjective_wellbeing_and_life_satisfaction
description: Measures hedonic and evaluative dimensions of personal wellbeing, including overall happiness, life satisfaction, perceived control over one's future, and confidence in realizing personal projects.

name: socioeconomic_mobility_and_opportunity_perception
description: Assesses respondents' perceptions of economic progress across generations and their expectations about future opportunity, reflecting beliefs about social mobility and structural fairness in Mexico.

name: moral_conservatism_and_social_values
description: Taps into traditional versus liberal value orientations across family, gender, and ethical domains, including attitudes toward corporal punishment, premarital sex, abortion, same-sex adoption, and self-placement on a conservative-liberal scale.
